entries.title: Agents
entries.body: Agents are the core actors of the NeuroVerse: humans, devices, sensors, drones, vehicles, AI models, robots, or any entity capable of perceiving and acting. Systems emerge from their interactions.

entries.title: State & Global State
entries.body: State is what an agent currently believes about the world. Global State is the shared belief across the network. When these diverge—systems drift. Alignment is the first law of stability.

entries.title: Latency
entries.body: Latency is the delay between cause and effect in real-world systems. High latency breaks coordination. Low latency creates flow. This principle governs AR, robots, networks, and leadership.

entries.title: The Posemesh
entries.body: The Posemesh is the spatial coordination layer. It aligns agents on shared time, space, and orientation, allowing AR objects to stay pinned, robots to navigate reliably, and the real-world web to form.

entries.title: Uploads
entries.body: Uploads are cognitive upgrades that teach you how to design, build, and lead real-world systems. Each upload activates new perception and pattern-recognition abilities.

entries.title: Missions
entries.body: Missions convert learning into action. Each mission produces a behavior or artifact that strengthens the NeuroVerse through your contribution.
